title: In Utero Fetal-to-Fetal Kidney Transplantation in Rats
author: Keita Morimoto
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

steps.instruction: Sprague–Dawley (SD) pregnant rats (Sankyo Labo Service Corp., Japan)

steps.title: SD-Tg [CAG-enhanced green fluorescent protein (EGFP)]...
steps.instruction: SD-Tg [CAG-enhanced green fluorescent protein (EGFP)] pregnant rats (also called GFP-SD rats) (Sankyo Labo Service Corp., Japan) Reagents

steps.instruction: 5-0 silk suture (Natsume Seisakusho Co., catalog number: CF1250B2NT)

steps.title: 15–16 G needle (Saito Medical Instruments Inc
steps.instruction: 15–16 G needle (Saito Medical Instruments Inc.)

steps.title: 10 cm sterile culture dish (AS ONE Co., catalog number
steps.instruction: 10 cm sterile culture dish (AS ONE Co., catalog number: GD90-

steps.title: 1 mL syringe (TERUMO, catalog number
steps.instruction: 1 mL syringe (TERUMO, catalog number: SS-01T)

steps.title: Polystyrene box (size L260 mm ×...
steps.instruction: Polystyrene box (size L260 mm × H175 mm × W160 mm)

steps.title: Euthanize GFP-expressing pregnant Sprague–Dawley rats (E14
steps.instruction: Euthanize GFP-expressing pregnant Sprague–Dawley rats (E14.0–16.

steps.title: under isoflurane anesthesia by intravenous injection...
steps.instruction: under isoflurane anesthesia by intravenous injection of pentobarbital (120 mg/kg body weight).

steps.title: Incise the maternal skin and abdominal...
steps.instruction: Incise the maternal skin and abdominal wall with surgical scissors, excise the entire uterus with fetuses intact, and transfer it to a 10 cm dish filled with HBSS.

steps.title: Under a sterile stereomicroscope, open the...
steps.instruction: Under a sterile stereomicroscope, open the myometrium and amnion with ophthalmic scissors, sever each umbilical cord, and remove the fetuses one by one.

steps.title: After collecting all fetuses, place them...
steps.instruction: After collecting all fetuses, place them in a fresh 10 cm dish containing HBSS and keep it on ice.

steps.title: Under the stereomicroscope, isolate the metanephros–bladder...
steps.instruction: Under the stereomicroscope, isolate the metanephros–bladder units (MNBs) from each fetus.

steps.title: Transfer a single fetus to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer a single fetus to a new 10 cm dish filled with HBSS. Working at room temperature with two microforceps, dissect the MNB within 10–15 min (practice is required to achieve this speed).

steps.title: Decapitate the fetus, place it in...
steps.instruction: Decapitate the fetus, place it in the left lateral recumbent position ( Figure 1a ), and make a longitudinal incision in the right thoracoabdominal wall to expose the thoracic and abdominal cavities. Avoid damaging the visceral organs.

steps.title: Extend the incision toward the umbilicus
steps.instruction: Extend the incision toward the umbilicus. Trace the umbilical vessels—running alongside the bladder toward the common iliac arteries to locate the bladder ( Figure 1b ).

steps.title: Detach the connective tissue at the...
steps.instruction: Detach the connective tissue at the bladder neck and sever the rectum caudal to the bladder.

steps.title: Rotate the fetus into the right lateral recumbent position
steps.instruction: Rotate the fetus into the right lateral recumbent position.

steps.title: As in step A7, make a...
steps.instruction: As in step A7, make a longitudinal incision in the left thoracoabdominal wall and extend it toward the umbilicus.

steps.title: Peel the back, including the vertebral...
steps.instruction: Peel the back, including the vertebral column, away from the trunk, ensuring the abdominal aorta remains with the trunk to prevent detaching the kidneys. If the kidneys separate with the back, the bladder remains in the trunk, and the ureters will be severed ( Figure 1c ).

steps.title: After peeling away the back, excise...
steps.instruction: After peeling away the back, excise the bilateral mesonephroi and adrenal glands ( Figure 1d ).

steps.title: Transect the abdominal aorta proximal to...
steps.instruction: Transect the abdominal aorta proximal to the kidneys along with the superior mesenteric artery and excise the kidneys and bladder en bloc.

steps.title: Remove any extraneous tissue from the...
steps.instruction: Remove any extraneous tissue from the harvested MNBs, leaving only the kidneys and bladder.

steps.title: Place the harvested MNBs ( Figure...
steps.instruction: Place the harvested MNBs ( Figure 1e ) in a fresh 10 cm dish containing HBSS on ice and keep them until transplantation. Perform transplantation within 1 h of harvest. Video

steps.title: Dissection and retrieval of the metanephros–bladder...
steps.instruction: Dissection and retrieval of the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB) from Sprague–Dawley rat fetuses (E15.

steps.title: Isolation of the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB)...
steps.instruction: Isolation of the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB) from fetal Sprague–Dawley rats (E15.5). (a) Left lateral recumbent position of an E15.5 Sprague–Dawley fetus. (b) Identification of the bladder after thoracoabdominal incision. (c) Peeling the dorsal body wall, including the vertebral column, away from the trunk while ensuring the abdominal aorta remains attached to the trunk. (d) Bilateral mesonephroi and adrenal glands prior to excision. (e) Excised MNB. B. Loading the harvested MNBs onto the needle

steps.title: Attach a 1-mL syringe to a 15–16 G needle. Note
steps.instruction: Attach a 1-mL syringe to a 15–16 G needle. Note: Use a custom thin-walled needle supplied by Saito Medical Instruments Inc., as a 15–16 G needle is the narrowest gauge that can accommodate the fetal kidney within the needle lumen.

steps.title: Fill a 10-cm dish nearly to...
steps.instruction: Fill a 10-cm dish nearly to the brim with HBSS to minimize air entry into the needle during loading.

steps.title: Conduct all loading procedures under a stereomicroscope
steps.instruction: Conduct all loading procedures under a stereomicroscope.

steps.title: Transfer the harvested MNBs to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the harvested MNBs to the HBSS-filled dish prepared in step B

steps.title: Fully submerge the bevel tip of...
steps.instruction: Fully submerge the bevel tip of the 15–16 G needle in HBSS and gently retract the plunger to fill the needle lumen, avoiding air bubbles. Approximately 0.1–0.2 mL of HBSS is sufficient. Keep the needle tip submerged throughout.

steps.title: Under a stereoscopic view, bring the...
steps.instruction: Under a stereoscopic view, bring the MNB and needle tip into the same field.

steps.title: Hold the needle with the left...
steps.instruction: Hold the needle with the left hand and use microforceps in the right hand to gently guide the MNB—bladder end first—into the needle lumen.

steps.title: Once both kidneys have entered the...
steps.instruction: Once both kidneys have entered the lumen, retract the plunger an additional 0.1 mL (approximately) so that the MNB is just inside the bevel. Avoid over-aspiration to prevent tissue damage (See Video 2 for steps B7–8).

steps.title: After loading, remove the needle from...
steps.instruction: After loading, remove the needle from the HBSS and place it horizontally in a sterile container.

steps.title: To prevent desiccation, cover the needle...
steps.instruction: To prevent desiccation, cover the needle tip with sterile gauze moistened with HBSS.

steps.title: Keep the loaded needle at room...
steps.instruction: Keep the loaded needle at room temperature (22–27 °C); MNBs remain viable for up to 1 h post-loading. Video

steps.title: Loading the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB) onto...
steps.instruction: Loading the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB) onto the tip of a 15–16 G needle C. Transuterine transplantation

steps.title: Perform all transplantations under a stereomicroscope;...
steps.instruction: Perform all transplantations under a stereomicroscope; place a 37 °C warming pad beneath the stage.

steps.title: Anesthetize pregnant Sprague–Dawley rats (E18
steps.instruction: Anesthetize pregnant Sprague–Dawley rats (E18.0–18.

steps.title: with isoflurane and position them supine on the warming pad
steps.instruction: with isoflurane and position them supine on the warming pad.

steps.title: Shave the abdomen, taking care not...
steps.instruction: Shave the abdomen, taking care not to injure the nipples ( Figure 2a ).

steps.title: Disinfect the abdominal skin with 70%...
steps.instruction: Disinfect the abdominal skin with 70% ethanol and drape with sterile gauze that has a central opening.

steps.title: Make a midline laparotomy
steps.instruction: Make a midline laparotomy. Using ring forceps, gently exteriorize the uterus by grasping the uterine wall between fetuses; avoid contact with uterine vessels to prevent bleeding and disruption of fetal blood flow ( Video 3 ).

steps.title: Once the entire uterus is externalized,...
steps.instruction: Once the entire uterus is externalized, cover it with sterile gauze moistened with 37 °C HBSS to prevent drying. The thin uterine wall allows visualization of individual fetuses ( Figure 2b ,c).

steps.title: Select recipient fetuses that are clearly...
steps.instruction: Select recipient fetuses that are clearly visible, with the head oriented to the left of the dam and the tail to the right. Transplantation is still feasible, however, even when the fetal orientation is reversed ( Figure 2c ).

steps.title: Hold the ring forceps in the...
steps.instruction: Hold the ring forceps in the left hand and the 15–16 G needle in the right hand and puncture.

steps.title: Under stereoscopic vision, stabilize the target...
steps.instruction: Under stereoscopic vision, stabilize the target fetus and uterus with the ring forceps.

steps.title: Pierce the cranial uterine wall and...
steps.instruction: Pierce the cranial uterine wall and amniotic membrane with the needle.

steps.title: Lower the needle almost parallel to...
steps.instruction: Lower the needle almost parallel to the table and puncture the superficial lateral thoracic region of the fetus.

steps.title: After skin penetration, advance the needle approximately 1 cm subcutaneously
steps.instruction: After skin penetration, advance the needle approximately 1 cm subcutaneously.

steps.title: Release the ring forceps, grasp the...
steps.instruction: Release the ring forceps, grasp the needle with the left hand to immobilize it, and depress the plunger by 0.1 mL to expel the HBSS and the MNB (see Videos 4 and 5 for steps C8–13).

steps.title: Observe a subcutaneous bulge filled with HBSS under the microscope
steps.instruction: Observe a subcutaneous bulge filled with HBSS under the microscope.

steps.title: Withdraw the needle and confirm GFP...
steps.instruction: Withdraw the needle and confirm GFP fluorescence to verify the correct placement of the GFP-positive MNB beneath the fetal skin. Proper subcutaneous placement causes minimal fetal bleeding; the uterine puncture hole is left unsutured ( Figure 2d ).

steps.title: Repeat the procedure in 1–4 fetuses,...
steps.instruction: Repeat the procedure in 1–4 fetuses, limiting the total operative time (from anesthesia induction to closure) to ≤ 40 min; the exact timing depends on the operator’s skill. The duration of anesthesia is approximately 30 min.

steps.title: Return the uterus to the abdominal cavity with ring forceps
steps.instruction: Return the uterus to the abdominal cavity with ring forceps. Ensure no torsion, especially near the cervix, to avoid obstructing parturition.

steps.title: Instill 5 mL of 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Instill 5 mL of 37 °C HBSS into the peritoneal cavity to reduce postoperative adhesions.

steps.title: Close the abdomen with 5–0 silk
steps.instruction: Close the abdomen with 5–0 silk.

steps.title: Discontinue anesthesia and return the dam to its home cage
steps.instruction: Discontinue anesthesia and return the dam to its home cage. Video

steps.title: Gentle exteriorization of the uterus from pregnant Sprague–Dawley rats (E18
steps.instruction: Gentle exteriorization of the uterus from pregnant Sprague–Dawley rats (E18.

steps.title: Transuterine transplantation of the metanephros–bladder unit...
steps.instruction: Transuterine transplantation of the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB) into the subcutaneous space of E18.5 fetal Sprague–Dawley rats using a 16 G needle (macroscopic view) Video

steps.title: Microscopic view of transuterine transplantation of...
steps.instruction: Microscopic view of transuterine transplantation of the metanephros–bladder unit (MNB) into the subcutaneous space of E18.5 fetal Sprague–Dawley rats using a 16 G needle Figure

steps.title: Transuterine transplantation of fetal metanephros–bladder units (MNBs) into recipient fetuses
steps.instruction: Transuterine transplantation of fetal metanephros–bladder units (MNBs) into recipient fetuses. (a) Pregnant Sprague–Dawley rat (E18.

steps.title: after abdominal shaving
steps.instruction: after abdominal shaving. (b) Entire uterus exteriorized and covered with sterile gauze moistened with 37 °C HBSS. (c) Thin uterine wall allows visualization of individual fetuses. (d) GFP-positive MNB visualized beneath the fetal skin immediately after transplantation. (e) GFP-positive transplanted MNB detected subcutaneously in the neonate after birth. Scale bars: 2 mm in (d) and (e). GFP, green fluorescent protein. Panels d and e are reproduced from Figure 1f and Figure 2c of [9], licensed under CC BY 4.0 ( https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ ). D. Observation of the neonates

steps.title: Four days later (gestational day 22), allow natural delivery
steps.instruction: Four days later (gestational day 22), allow natural delivery.

steps.title: Briefly remove the pups from the...
steps.instruction: Briefly remove the pups from the cage and identify the transplanted individuals by GFP fluorescence under a stereomicroscope ( Figure 2e ).

description: Validation of protocol This protocol (or parts of it) has been used and validated in the following research article: Keita et al. [9]. Fetal-to-Fetal Kidney Transplantation in Utero Communications Biology (Figures 1–2 and Table 1). In the preceding study, this method was used to compare immune rejection and the degree of fetal kidney maturation between fetal-to-fetal transplantation and transplantation into adult recipients, using maturation markers such as nephrin (podocytes), LTL (proximal tubules), and E-cadherin (distal tubules). For figures and videos relevant to this protocol, please refer to Figure 1 and Supplementary Video 1 in the cited reference [9]. The transplantation success rates shown in Table 1 of this protocol are reproduced from Table 1 of [9], licensed under CC BY 4.0 ( https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ ). Table 1. Transplant success rate [9] *The difficulty of the intrauterine transplantation procedure varies depending on the orientation and size of the fetus and the amount of amniotic fluid. In pregnant rat number 4, the transplantation technique was challenging, requiring extended time to transplant into a single fetus. To avoid prolonged anesthesia time, we completed the procedure after transplanting into just one fetus. In contrast, in pregnant rat number 2, the transplantation procedure was relatively straightforward, allowing us to successfully transplant into four fetuses. Pregnant rat number Number of live pups/all fetuses Survival rate (%) Number of transplanted fetuses Number of GFP-positive neonates Success rate (%) 1 12/14 86 2 1 50 2 9/11 82 4* 4 100 3 11/14 79 2 2 100 4 8/14 57 1* 1 100 Average - 76 - - 88
